name: Paracetamol
usage: Pain reliever and fever reducer. Used for headaches, muscle aches, arthritis, backaches, toothaches, colds, and fevers.
dosage: Adults: 500mg-1000mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum: 4000mg per day. Take with water.
warning: Do not exceed recommended dose. Avoid alcohol while taking this medication. Consult doctor if pregnant.

name: Dolo
usage: Pain reliever and fever reducer. Used for headaches, muscle aches, arthritis, backaches, toothaches, colds, and fevers.
dosage: Adults: 500mg-1000mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum: 4000mg per day. Take with water.
warning: Do not exceed recommended dose. Avoid alcohol while taking this medication. Consult doctor if pregnant.

name: Calpol
usage: Pain reliever and fever reducer. Used for headaches, muscle aches, arthritis, backaches, toothaches, colds, and fevers.
dosage: Adults: 500mg-1000mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum: 4000mg per day. Take with water.
warning: Do not exceed recommended dose. Avoid alcohol while taking this medication. Consult doctor if pregnant.

name: Ibuprofen
usage: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used for pain relief, reducing inflammation, and fever reduction.
dosage: Adults: 200mg-400mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum: 1200mg per day. Take with food or milk.
warning: Take with food or milk to avoid stomach upset. Not recommended for pregnant women. May cause drowsiness.

name: Amoxicillin
usage: Penicillin-type antibiotic used to treat various bacterial infections like pneumonia, bronchitis, and ear infections.
dosage: Adults: 250mg-500mg every 8 hours. Complete full course as prescribed by doctor.
warning: Complete full course even if symptoms improve. Avoid if allergic to penicillin. May cause diarrhea.

name: Aspirin
usage: Used to treat pain, fever, and inflammation. Also used for heart attack prevention in low doses.
dosage: Adults: 325mg-650mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum: 4000mg per day.
warning: Not for children with viral infections. May cause stomach bleeding. Avoid if you have bleeding disorders.

name: Cetirizine
usage: Antihistamine used to treat allergy symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, itchy eyes, and skin rashes.
dosage: Adults: 10mg once daily. Children: 5mg once daily. Can be taken with or without food.
warning: May cause drowsiness. Avoid alcohol. Use caution when driving or operating machinery.

name: Metformin
usage: Used to treat type 2 diabetes mellitus. Helps control blood sugar levels.
dosage: Adults: 500mg twice daily with meals. Dosage may be adjusted by doctor.
warning: Take with meals to reduce stomach upset. Monitor blood sugar regularly. Avoid alcohol.

name: Azithromycin
usage: Macrolide antibiotic used to treat respiratory infections, skin infections, and ear infections.
dosage: Adults: 500mg once daily for 5 days. Take one hour before or two hours after meals.
warning: Complete full course. May cause stomach upset. Do not take with antacids.

name: Omeprazole
usage: Used to treat acid reflux, GERD, stomach ulcers, and heartburn.
dosage: Adults: 20mg once daily before breakfast. Swallow whole, do not crush.
warning: Take before meals. Long-term use may cause vitamin B12 deficiency. Consult doctor for long-term use.

name: Levocetirizine
usage: Second-generation antihistamine for treating allergic rhinitis and chronic urticaria.
dosage: Adults: 5mg once daily in the evening. Children above 6 years: 5mg once daily.
warning: May cause drowsiness. Avoid alcohol. Not recommended for pregnant women.

name: Atorvastatin
usage: Used to lower cholesterol and reduce risk of heart attack and stroke.
dosage: Adults: 10mg-20mg once daily. Take at the same time each day.
warning: Report muscle pain or weakness immediately. Avoid grapefruit. Monitor liver function.
